title: Cat Urinating Frequent Small Amounts: Triage and Emergency Guide
meta_desc: Is your cat urinating frequently in small amounts? This is a high-priority emergency. Learn what it means and why you must act now.
content_html:
What is frequent urination in cats?

When a cat makes repeated, urgent trips to the litter box only to produce tiny amounts of urine, it is often a sign of Lower Urinary Tract Disease (FLUTD). This behavior, known as pollakiuria, suggests that the bladder lining is irritated, inflamed, or that there is a physical obstruction preventing the bladder from emptying normally.

Is this a veterinary emergency?

Urgency Level: High. This condition should be treated as a medical emergency, especially in male cats. If your cat is straining and producing no urine at all, they may have a life-threatening urethral blockage. A blockage can lead to kidney failure and heart issues within hours. Even if some urine is passing, the intense pain and risk of a full obstruction require immediate veterinary evaluation.

Why a photo helps with triage

If it is safe to do so, taking a clear photo of the litter box can be incredibly helpful for your veterinarian. A photo allows the medical team to see the exact volume of the urine spots, the color (checking for blood), and whether there are visible crystals or mucus plugs. This visual evidence helps the vet prioritize your cat's care the moment you walk through the door.

faqs.question: Can I wait until morning to see my regular vet?
faqs.answer: If your cat is straining, vocalizing in pain, or unable to produce urine, you should not wait. Seek care at an emergency animal hospital immediately, as urinary blockages are fatal if left untreated.

faqs.question: Are male cats more at risk than females?
faqs.answer: Yes. While both can suffer from urinary issues, male cats have a narrower urethra, making them significantly more prone to life-threatening physical obstructions.

faqs.question: What are other signs of a urinary emergency?
faqs.answer: Look for lethargy, loss of appetite, vomiting, or excessive licking of the genital area. These signs, combined with frequent small urinations, indicate a severe medical issue.
